rationale.R1.1: Artifact presents a thesis (BiasFlow debiases LLM annotator preferences) and a specific contribution claim (non-parametric prior over latent preferences); verifying conceptual coherence of these is central to any plausible reading of 'check this'.
rationale.R1.2: The abstract mixes probabilistic modeling language with algorithmic claims — e.g., it asserts Beta-Bernoulli closed-form conjugacy for z while simultaneously using SVI for theta, and claims 'no parametric assumptions' while specifying a Beta prior. Checking logical consistency of the model specification is load-bearing.
rationale.R1.3: Two quantitative claims are made (38% rank-inversion reduction; Spearman rho=0.91 vs. human preferences) without referencing a table, figure, or appendix. Verifying whether these claims are traceable to evidence is load-bearing under a technical reading of 'check'.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on technical and logical correctness of the model specification and quantitative claims (R1.2/R1.3 load-bearing), or primarily on writing clarity and prose quality (R1.6 load-bearing)?
